slides.title: Mouth-breathing is quietly wrecking your *sleep*.
slides.sub: It's not just snoring. The way you breathe all day is shaping your nights — and your calm. Here's why.

slides.title: Your nose does more
slides.body: Nasal breathing raises *CO2 tolerance and nitric oxide*, which helps your blood actually deliver the oxygen it carries.

slides.title: Breathe through your nose
slides.body: Mouth-breathing drives snoring, dry mouth, and lighter sleep. *Nose-breathing at night* can support deeper rest — ask a doctor before taping.

slides.title: Slow it down
slides.body: Slow nasal breaths flip on your *rest-and-digest state*, which may help trade scattered energy for calmer, steadier focus.

slides.title: Train the tolerance
slides.body: Nose-breathe on easy walks and slow runs to *build CO2 tolerance*. Start gentle and let the pace come back over weeks.

slides.title: Mind your tongue
slides.body: Rest your *tongue on the roof* of your mouth between breaths. It supports a more open airway, day and night.

slides.title: Better breathing, *deeper sleep*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep and ties it to how you feel, so the nights you breathe better show up on your trend line.

caption:
Mouth-breathing is quietly wrecking your sleep — and it's not just the snoring. 😮‍💨

Nose-breathe at night, slow it down for focus, train the tolerance on easy walks, rest your tongue on the roof of your mouth. Save this one. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks your sleep and ties it to how you feel → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Nose Breathing Fixes Your Sleep and Focus
